title: Prepare a live metrics view for the spike

blocks.content:
A postmortem view hides how fast load ramps feel in real time. Before you click **Run** on the spike test, stage a layout you can watch without hunting menus while virtual users climb.

You can keep everything in **Explore** with two queries (one `grafanacloud-k6`, one metrics data source) as in the previous milestone, or build a short-lived dashboard so stakeholders see k6 beside system metrics during a POV. Refer to [Correlate results in Grafana](/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/analyze-results/correlate-results-in-grafana/) for **Explore** and **Copy to Clipboard** from Grafana Cloud k6.

To prepare the live view in **Explore**, complete the following steps.

1. Open the **Explore** layout you will keep visible during the spike.

1. Confirm one query shows virtual users (or another generator-side metric you care about) from `grafanacloud-k6` for the Performance **Project** you use in this lab.

1. Click **Add query** and select your Prometheus-compatible data source.

1. Run PromQL for quickpizza request volume or error rate using the label filters you validated in the previous milestone.

1. Dock the Grafana tab beside your Grafana Cloud k6 tab, or arrange windows so you can see **Run** progress and both metrics at once.

1. Set the time range to **Last 15 minutes** or similar so the chart updates quickly when the test starts.

   You tighten the absolute window after the run in later milestones.

1. Write one sentence in your notes describing what you expect to see when load ramps.

   You compare that expectation to the live curves during the spike.

Optional: add k6 and system metrics to a dashboard for a POV. Complete the following steps if your audience expects a saved dashboard, not only **Explore**.

1. Open the Grafana Cloud k6 **Test Result** view for a recent run (or the project you use for this lab).

1. On a k6 metric panel, open the actions menu (three dots) and choose **Copy to Clipboard**.

   Refer to [Correlate results in Grafana](/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/analyze-results/correlate-results-in-grafana/) for screenshots of the menu.

1. Open the dashboard you use for the demo service (create a new dashboard if you need a scratch space).

1. Click **Add**, then **Paste panel**, and save the dashboard.

1. Click **Add**, then **Visualization**, and select your Prometheus-compatible data source.

1. Add one panel for quickpizza request rate or errors using the PromQL you validated earlier.

   Place it beside the k6 panel so both series share one time axis during the spike.

1. Set the dashboard time picker to **Last 15 minutes** (or a range that will cover the spike) before you click **Run** in Grafana Cloud k6.

Check your work: you can move on when both signals render on screen without errors, your layout stays visible while you operate Grafana Cloud k6, and you captured the expectation note. If panels fail after refresh, reopen **Explore** or the dashboard, reselect data sources, then open the troubleshooting topics for this milestone.

In the next milestone you run the spike script, keep this view visible while the test executes, and record the UTC start time.
